task_completion.visual_evidence: A Microsoft Edge browser window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The browser has navigated to the Wikipedia main page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser's address bar shows 'wikipedia.org' (or 'https://www.wikipedia.org') and the main Wikipedia portal page is displayed in the viewport.
The 'Favorite added' or 'Add favorite' popup dialog is visible, typically dropping down from the star icon in the address bar.

task_completion.description: The bookmark is set to be saved in the 'Favorites bar' or 'Other favorites' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the bookmark popup dialog, the 'Folder' dropdown explicitly displays either 'Favorites bar' or 'Other favorites'.
The star icon in the address bar is filled/solid (indicating the page is bookmarked), or the Wikipedia link is visibly present on the browser's Favorites bar or inside the Other favorites menu.

integrity.description: The agent used Microsoft Edge, not a different browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser UI matches Microsoft Edge (e.g., Edge-specific icons, menus, or window title), rather than Chrome, Firefox, or another application.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to add the bookmark.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the browser UI (like clicking the star icon) or the bookmark dialog appearing, rather than a terminal window executing background scripts.

integrity.description: The agent loaded the real Wikipedia website over the internet.
integrity.visual_evidence: The address bar shows a standard HTTPS URL for Wikipedia, not a local file path (e.g., 'file://') or a localhost address.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target URL is 'https://www.wikipedia.org/'. The expected bookmark domain pattern is 'wikipedia.org'. The expected bookmark title starts with 'Wikipedia' (actual title: 'Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia'). The task requires using Microsoft Edge and saving the bookmark specifically to the 'Favorites bar' or 'Other favorites' folder.
